message.content: Analyze Command System Layer (192 files): Extract all REPL commands (class, space, hprof, backtrace, thread, mmap), command registration patterns, remote debugging support. Document command interfaces and data flow.

attachment.content:
- coredump: Analyze an Android coredump file. Print a Java class and its members, or drill into any field by address.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- superpowers:brainstorm: Deprecated - use the superpowers:brainstorming skill instead
- superpowers:execute-plan: Deprecated - use the superpowers:executing-plans skill instead
- superpowers:write-plan: Deprecated - use the superpowers:writing-plans skill instead
- superpowers:brainstorming
- superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- superpowers:executing-plans
- superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch
- superpowers:receiving-code-review
- superpowers:requesting-code-review
- superpowers:subagent-driven-development
- superpowers:systematic-debugging
- superpowers:test-driven-development
- superpowers:using-git-worktrees
- superpowers:using-superpowers
- superpowers:verification-before-completion
- superpowers:writing-plans
- superpowers:writing-skills
- claude-mem:babysit
- claude-mem:design-is
- claude-mem:do
- claude-mem:how-it-works
- claude-mem:knowledge-agent
- claude-mem:learn-codebase
- claude-mem:make-plan
- claude-mem:mem-search
- claude-mem:oh-my-issues
- claude-mem:pathfinder
- claude-mem:smart-explore
- claude-mem:timeline-report
- claude-mem:version-bump
- claude-mem:weekly-digests
- claude-mem:wowerpoint
- understand-anything:understand
- understand-anything:understand-chat
- understand-anything:understand-dashboard
- understand-anything:understand-diff
- understand-anything:understand-domain
- understand-anything:understand-explain
- understand-anything:understand-knowledge
- understand-anything:understand-onboard
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review
- security-review

content.text: I'll analyze the Command System Layer by examining the command structure, registration patterns, and interfaces.

content.text:
# Command System Layer Analysis

## Overview
The Command System Layer implements a plugin-based REPL (Read-Eval-Print Loop) architecture with 179 source files providing core debugging commands, Android-specific analysis tools, and remote debugging capabilities.

## Core Architecture

### 1. Command Base Class (`/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/command.h`)

**Interface:**
```cpp
class Command {
    virtual int prepare(int argc, char* const argv[]);  // Pre-execution validation
    virtual int main(int argc, char* const argv[]) = 0; // Command implementation
    virtual int exitMain(int argc, char* const argv[]); // Post-execution cleanup
    virtual void usage() = 0;                           // Help text
    int execute(int argc, char* const argv[]);          // Execution wrapper
};
```

**Return Codes:**
- `FINISH (0)`: Command completed, continue REPL
- `CONTINUE (1)`: Command needs further processing
- `ONCHLD (2)`: Command spawns child process
- `ERREXIT (10)`: Fatal error, exit process

### 2. Command Manager (`/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/command_manager.cpp`)

**Registration System:**
- **Inline Commands**: Built-in commands registered at startup via `PushInlineCommand()`
- **Extend Commands**: Plugin-loaded commands via `PushExtendCommand()`
- **Command Discovery**: `FindCommand()` searches by name or shortcut

**Command Registration (Init()):**
```
Core Commands: core, exec, sysroot, mmap, auxv, file, linkmap, read, write, 
               register, thread, cs, vtor, ptov, ptype, backtrace, frame, disassemble

Android Commands: getprop, print, reference, hprof, search, class, top, space, 
                  dex, method, logcat, dumpsys, fdtrack

LLVM Commands: cxx, scudo
System Commands: env, ini, shell, plugin, help, remote, fake, time, version
```

**Execution Flow:**
1. Parse command line for pipe (`|`) or redirect (`>`)
2. If piped: Run through `ShellCommand` with callback
3. If redirected: Redirect stdout to file
4. Execute command via `command->execute(argc, argv)`

### 3. Key REPL Commands

#### Android Analysis Commands

**class** (`/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/android/cmd_class.cpp`)
- **Purpose**: Search and inspect Java class definitions
- **Options**: `-m` (methods), `-s` (static fields), `-f` (instance fields), `-i` (implements), `-x` (hex format)
- **Type Filters**: `--app`, `--zygote`, `--image`, `--fake`
- **Data Flow**: Iterates heap objects via `Android::ForeachObjects()` → filters by class name → pretty-prints class structure

**space** (`/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/android/cmd_space.cpp`)
- **Purpose**: List Java heap memory regions (continuous/discontinuous spaces)
- **Options**: `-c` (check for bad objects), `-f` (full verification)
- **Output**: Space type, address range, name (e.g., "main space (region space)", boot.art)
- **Verification**: Runs `JavaVerify` to detect corrupted objects, method conflicts, DEX pointer reuse

**hprof** (`/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/android/cmd_hprof.cpp`)
- **Purpose**: Dump Java heap to HPROF format for MAT/Android Studio analysis
- **Options**: `-v` (verbose), `-q` (quick mode)
- **Output**: Binary HPROF file compatible with standard heap analyzers
- **Data Flow**: Walks heap via `art::hprof::DumpHeap()` → serializes objects to HPROF format

**top** (`/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/android/cmd_top.h`)
- **Purpose**: Show top N classes by allocation count, shallow size, or native size
- **Sort Modes**: `ORDERBY_ALLOC`, `ORDERBY_SHALLOW`, `ORDERBY_NATIVE`
- **Use Case**: Memory profiling to identify heavy allocators

#### Core Debugging Commands

**mmap** (`/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/core/cmd_mmap.cpp`)
- **Purpose**: Map external files to core memory regions for symbol resolution
- **Syntax**: `mmap <ADDR> [<PATH>] -o <OFFSET>`
- **Options**: `--remove-mmap`, `--remove-overlay`
- **Data Flow**: Updates `LoadBlock` metadata → triggers `LinkMap::ReadSymbols()`

**thread** (`/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/core/cmd_thread.h`)
- **Purpose**: List and filter threads
- **Options**: `--native`, `--java`, `--dump-all`
- **Shortcut**: `t`

**backtrace** (`/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/core/backtrace/cmd_backtrace.h`)
- **Purpose**: Display call stacks (native, JVM, JNI)
- **Thread Types**: `TYPE_NATIVE`, `TYPE_JVM`
- **Data Flow**: Reads register context → unwinds frames → resolves symbols
- **Shortcut**: `bt`

**cs (CoreSearch)** (`/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/core/cmd_search.h`)
- **Purpose**: Search memory for byte patterns
- **Modes**: Stack search (`--in-stack`) or full memory walk
- **Data Flow**: `WalkStack()` or `WalkLoadBlock()` with pattern matching

### 4. Remote Debugging Support

**remote** (`/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/remote/cmd_remote.cpp`)

**Subcommands:**
- `core`: Dump live process via `Opencore::Dump()` using ptrace
- `hook`: Inject hooks via `Hook::Main()`
- `rd`: Read memory from `/proc/<pid>/mem`
- `wd`: Write memory to `/proc/<pid>/mem`
- `pause`: Stop threads via `StopTheWorld()` or `StopTheThread()`
- `maps`: Parse `/proc/<pid>/maps`
- `setprop`: Android property manipulation (Android only)

**Remote Operations:**
```cpp
RemoteCommand::Read(pid, vaddr, size, buf);   // pread64 from /proc/pid/mem
RemoteCommand::Write(pid, vaddr, buf, size);  // pwrite64 to /proc/pid/mem
```

**Platform**: Linux only (`!defined(__MACOS__) && !defined(__WINDOWS__)`)

### 5. Plugin System

**PluginCommand** (`/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/cmd_plugin.h`)
- **Dynamic Loading**: Load `.so` plugins via `dlopen()`
- **Plugin Lifecycle**: `Load()` → `HookCommand()` → `UnLoad()`
- **Command Hooking**: Plugins register extend commands via `CommandManager::PushExtendCommand()`
- **Collision Detection**: Prevents duplicate command names

### 6. Server Modes

#### HTTP Server (`/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/daemon/http/server.h`)
- **Launch**: `core-parser --http [IP:PORT]`
- **Default**: `127.0.0.1:1104`
- **Use Case**: REST API for remote analysis

#### MCP Server (`/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/daemon/mcp/server.h`)
- **Launch**: `core-parser --mcp`
- **Protocol**: JSON-RPC over stdio
- **Use Case**: AI assistant integration (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.)
- **Startup Behavior**: Suppresses diagnostic output to `/dev/null` during preload to preserve stdio channel

### 7. Command Execution Pipeline

**Main Loop** (`/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/core_parser.cpp`):
```
1. CommandManager::Init()              // Register all commands
2. UiThread::GetCommand(&cmdline)      // Read input via mini_shell
3. WorkThread(cmdline)                 // Parse and execute in thread
4. CommandManager::Execute(cmd, argc, argv)
5. Command::prepare() → main() → exitMain()
6. UiThread::Wake()                    // Signal completion
```

**WorkThread** (`/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/work/work_thread.h`):
- Tokenizes command line (space/tab/newline delimiters)
- Spawns thread for async execution
- MAX_ARGC = 16 arguments

### 8. Command Data Flow Patterns

**Pattern 1: Heap Iteration**
```
class/top/search → Android::ForeachObjects(callback, flags) 
                 → Runtime::GetHeap() → Walk ContinuousSpaces/DiscontinuousSpaces
                 → Callback per object
```

**Pattern 2: Symbol Resolution**
```
backtrace/frame → ThreadApi::UnwindStack() 
                → LinkMap::FindSymbol(pc)
                → LoadBlock::ReadSymbols() from ELF
```

**Pattern 3: Remote Debugging**
```
remote core -p <pid> → Opencore::StopTheWorld(pid) 
                     → ptrace(PTRACE_ATTACH) all threads
                     → Read /proc/<pid>/mem + /proc/<pid>/maps
                     → FakeCore::Write() → ELF core file
```

## Command Interface Summary

| Category | Commands | Purpose |
|----------|----------|---------|
| **Android Java** | class, space, hprof, top, search, print, reference, method, dex | Heap analysis, class inspection, object search |
| **Android System** | getprop, logcat, dumpsys, fdtrack | System properties, logs, service dumps, FD tracking |
| **Core Memory** | mmap, read, write, auxv, file, linkmap, ptov, vtor, ptype | Memory mapping, symbol resolution, address translation |
| **Stack/Thread** | backtrace, frame, thread, register | Call stacks, register inspection, thread listing |
| **Disassembly** | disassemble, cs | Instruction disassembly, memory search |
| **Remote Debug** | remote (core/hook/rd/wd/pause/maps) | Live process attachment, memory read/write |
| **LLVM** | cxx, scudo | C++ runtime, Scudo allocator analysis |
| **System** | env, ini, shell, plugin, help, time, version | Configuration, plugin management, shell integration |
| **Fake Core** | fake | Convert tombstone → core file |

## Key Files

**Command Infrastructure:**
- `/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/command.h` - Base interface
- `/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/command_manager.cpp` - Registration/dispatch
- `/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/core_parser.cpp` - Main entry point

**Critical Commands:**
- `/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/android/cmd_class.cpp` - Java class inspector
- `/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/android/cmd_space.cpp` - Heap region viewer
- `/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/android/cmd_hprof.cpp` - HPROF exporter
- `/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/core/cmd_mmap.cpp` - Memory mapping
- `/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/core/backtrace/cmd_backtrace.h` - Stack unwinding
- `/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/command/remote/cmd_remote.cpp` - Remote debugging

**Server Modes:**
- `/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/daemon/http/server.h` - HTTP REST API
- `/home/cwtrocks/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/parser/daemon/mcp/server.h` - MCP stdio protocol
